channel.title: Recommendation System on DAXPY
channel.description: Recent content in Recommendation System on DAXPY

item.title: Collaborative Filtering Part 03
item.description:
Collaborative Filtering Part 03 - Scaling with Features & Neural Networks

In Part 2, we ended with the cold start problem. WMF and LMF are powerful, but they are built entirely on interaction history. No interactions means no recommendations. A new user or a new item is effectively invisible to these models.

But cold start is only one symptom of a deeper issue.

The Scaling Wall

Pure CF models — MF, WMF, LMF — each learn one vector per user and one vector per item. The entire model is a lookup table. Given a user ID, retrieve $p_u$. Given an item ID, retrieve $q_i$. Dot product. Done.

item.title: Collaborative Filtering Part 02
item.description:
Collaborative Filtering Part 02 - The “Zero” Problem - Solving for Implicit Data

In Part 1, we discussed predicting $P(click)$ using an interaction matrix. However, we concluded that traditional matrix factorization is not viable when the matrix contains empty cells.

Our approach should account for the uncertainty of probability of a click for those empty cells. So we pivot: instead of trying to factorize a full matrix, we treat this as an Optimisation Problem over the data we actually have.

item.title: Collaborative Filtering Part 01
item.description:
Collaborative Filtering - The Foundations & Classic Flaws

In the world of recommendation-system, the main objective simple to state but hard to do:

Figure out which content to recommend to which user.

The easiest strategy is to show the most popular items and hope for the best. But can we do better?

This means figuring out which content a particular user is likely to enjoy. Not just what the crowd likes. That sounds straightforward until we hit two hard realities:
